prompt:
Pharmaceutical vial product photography, vertical 4:5 portrait composition. TWO REFERENCE IMAGES are provided. The FIRST reference is the LABEL / TYPOGRAPHY / CAP / BAR ANCHOR — a 3ml CLEAR-glass BPC-157 baseline showing the canonical label layout, typography hierarchy, navy cap (hex #101850), bottom accent bar, AND CRUCIALLY THE LABEL WRAP — note how the white label EXTENDS NEARLY EDGE-TO-EDGE across the visible front face of the cylindrical vial, wrapping the cylinder rather than floating as a small centered rectangle. Reproduce the label content, typeface, alignment, sizing, hairline, bar, AND label-wrap proportions EXACTLY from this first reference. The SECOND reference is the VIAL GLASS ANCHOR — an empty amber/dark-brown 3ml glass vial. Reproduce the vial's amber tinted glass color, geometry, and proportions EXACTLY from this second reference, including its short tubular shape and crimp neck — the vial must be the same compact 3ml size and silhouette as in the second reference, NOT taller or chunkier. Cap color must remain EXACTLY hex #101850 navy. Vial size: 3ml dark amber glass.

LABEL WRAP — CRITICAL, NON-NEGOTIABLE: the white label MUST extend horizontally across the full visible front face of the cylindrical vial body, from the visible left edge of the cylinder to the visible right edge. The label width must be AT LEAST 90 percent of the visible vial body width. The label looks like a paper strip wrapping the cylinder — NOT a small/medium rectangle floating on the center of the front. There must be NO substantial naked amber glass visible to the LEFT or RIGHT of the label in the same vertical band. Only a small natural curve / perspective falloff at the very edges where the label wraps around to the back of the cylinder is acceptable. Match the wrap proportions of the FIRST reference image exactly.

LABEL MATERIAL — CRITICAL: the label face must be rendered as pristine opaque matte white paper / vinyl, completely uniform across its entire surface. The label must be FULLY OPAQUE — absolutely NO translucency, NO see-through to the amber glass behind it. NO dark spots, NO low-opacity shadowy patches, NO amber tint bleeding through, NO blotches. Only natural soft photographic shadow at the curved edges is acceptable.

The label has the following content laid out top to bottom (exactly matching the first reference): (1) Upper roughly 70 percent of the label is a blank pristine white logo zone with absolutely no graphics, text, lines, or marks of any kind. (2) Product name 'BPC-157' rendered in bold weight 700 sans-serif (Helvetica Neue) in dark charcoal grey, perfectly horizontally centered, natural letter proportions. (3) Directly below with tight spacing, the dose-and-volume line 'xx mg · 3 ml' in bold weight 700 sans-serif at roughly 60 percent of the product-name size, charcoal grey, centered, single middle-dot separator. The 'xx' must be rendered LITERALLY as two lowercase letter x characters — intentional placeholder, NOT a number. (4) Below the dose with a small visual gap, two stacked compliance lines in thin weight 300 sans-serif, all-caps, medium grey. Line 1: 'FOR RESEARCH USE ONLY'. Line 2: 'NOT FOR HUMAN CONSUMPTION'. (5) Below the second compliance line, the lot line 'LOT: NH-1024' in thin weight 300 sans-serif, same small size, medium grey, single line, perfectly centered. (6) BAR: at the very bottom edge of the label, the slim solid navy accent bar at exactly hex #101850 must run UNINTERRUPTED EDGE-TO-EDGE across the FULL WIDTH of the label.

ABSOLUTELY NO additional hairlines, NO horizontal rules. Sharp focus, high resolution, clinical minimalist aesthetic on a clean studio white background. Do NOT render any font name or styling instruction (e.g. 'HELVETICA', 'BOLD', 'LIGHT', '700', '300') as visible text on the label. CRITICAL: render the dose line literally as 'xx mg · 3 ml' with two lowercase x's; do NOT replace 'xx' with a number. CRITICAL: label MUST wrap the visible front face of the cylinder — at least 90% of the vial body width.
